If you want the best, don’t call the rest! Call Paul Dombeck.
My wife was recently denied twice for SSDI (at the Initial and Reconsideration Level). She then requested a Hearing before an Administrative Law Judge and wanted attorney representation for that Level. We both then took on the task of finding an attorney to represent her. Between us, we called 15+ attorneys (local and national) to see if they would take my wife’s case. ALL 15+ attorneys declined because of the unique circumstances surrounding the case and my wife’s rare medical condition. We almost gave up and were very concerned my wife would have to go before a judge without legal representation. It was then we found Paul Dombeck by chance. He was highly knowledgeable of the SSDI claims process and compassionate about my wife’s medical condition. We dealt with both Paul and Tatiana leading up to the Hearing, and they were both quick to answer any questions we had. Paul did an excellent job explaining the Hearing process to put my wife’s mind at ease. Long story short, my wife received a Fully Favorable Decision, and that was all down to Paul and Tatiana. We both can’t thank them enough. If my wife and I ever have the need for an attorney in the future, Paul will be our go-to-guy!
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Mark. We're delighted to hear about your wife's successful SSDI case! Your trust in us is greatly appreciated, and we're here for you whenever you need legal support in the future!

I cannot say enough good things about Kate and the team at Simonds Law Group! Family court cases can be so hard emotionally and very stressful and I am so glad we had Kate on our side. My husband and I have hired her twice for two extremely important and difficult cases. Kate handled both cases with care and professionalism, she is communicative and understanding and guides her clients in the right direction every single time.

Our first case, four years ago, was an emotionally taxing and overwhelming custody battle and it was hard to see a light at the end of the tunnel. Kate's intelligence and hard work truly shined in this difficult to manage case and we ended up on top and granted with everything we had asked for from the Judge. She was there for us at almost any time of day when we needed her, quick to reply to any and all questions and broke down the legal process in a way we could understand. We knew then that more things may unfortunately arise in the future and that Kate would be our go to choice for representation.

Four years passed and here we are today, home from a hearing that went amazing because of Kate's hard work and dedication. Opposing council tried their hardest to put up roadblocks and make statements against us that just weren't true. They even attempted to create a narrative that included false allegations that could be considered criminal and Kate effortlessly navigates through it all. In this case we were able to settle before court and came out on top, again, being granted everything we hoped for.

I know Kate is someone my husband and I have hired to do a job but it is more than that. Having to re-visit the same issues again and again can be incredible difficult and I can't say enough about how lucky we are to have her as our lawyer.

I would absolutely recommend Kate and her law firm to anyone looking for a knowledgeable attorney who will always have your best interest in mind. If ever we need to re-visit this case in the future Kate will always be our go to who we trust implicitly.

I worked with her and the paralegal Barbara, who was also awesome, and throughout the entire process I felt like they really had my back. They are both very professional, precise on all filings, important task that need to be done, just the entire process I always felt like I was in good hands.

It’s important to be honest with your attorney, and I always felt like I could be myself and honest with her without being judged. Throughout the process she was my attorney, but there were times she was acting as my therapist as well. She never led me wrong throughout this tough time. I just really, really, really appreciate her for that.

Going through a custody battle for the first time is something no caring Mother or Father can ever be emotionally prepared for, so I made some mistakes at the beginning that could have really cost me. Kate never once led me on, or gave out false hope just to get more fees, which I’m sure most attorneys will do. At the beginning I felt like it was such an uphill battle, but in the end, I got literally everything that I wanted and I can’t thank Mrs. Simonds enough for that. She helped me and my son more than she will ever know.

She was amazing in the court room, amazing with communication, filings, just every aspect. I’ll just say this, I was extremely happy I had her on my side vs the attorney we were up against. She outshined him in every way throughout the entire process. I just always felt like we were one step ahead of the opposition. I really can’t thank her and her paralegal Barbara enough for all the hard work they did for me during the most important time of my life.

Hopefully those close to me never have to go through a divorce or child custody case, but I would without a doubt refer them to her if the time arises. Too honest sometimes, I was mad, upset at times. Family court is not fair. Kate forced my ex to switch lawyer cause the first one couldn’t find his head when he was talking to Kate. Her efficiency kept me on my toes as she does everything fast and speedy. She waits for nobody to get things done. The process is long and expensive but Kate ended up saving me thousands. I had to use my money where it really matter so we picked the fights we really wanted to win. We focus on that and now for the first time in 12 years I will have my kids this summer. I have nothing but gratitude towards my counsel, she coached me thru, she was available every time I needed her and even when I didn’t know I needed her. .the process of family court is grueling and painful, I was better off with Kate on my side.
